title: kube play should label the resources with similar labels than docker is using for compose
body:
### Feature request description

When running a compose project, we can inspect the containers and get the compose file used, and the context directory. (plus other informations)

This information is very valuable, as it allow to have more information on the source of the container(s).

Some of the labels compose is using are the following

```json
{
 "Labels": {
      "com.docker.compose.config-hash": "<hash>",
      "com.docker.compose.container-number": "1",
      "com.docker.compose.oneoff": "False",
      "com.docker.compose.project": "<project-name>",
      "com.docker.compose.project.config_files": "<compose.yaml path>",
      "com.docker.compose.project.working_dir": "<context-dir>",
  [...]
 }
}
```



### Suggest potential solution

Similar to the compose labels. E.g. `podman.kube.play: ""` etc.

### Have you considered any alternatives?

Manually annotate the pods.

The following is possible `podman kube play --annotation=foo=bar` but this is user dependant.

### Additional context

For compose, having this information allow us in Podman Desktop (using the Podman Quadlet extension) to have some specific actions, like generating quadlet

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0b442e9d-8a5a-4e68-b206-7d97c08e2716)

We can provide this action because we are able to extract the compose file from the running resources, and use [podlet](https://github.com/containers/podlet) to convert it and create a quadlet from it.

> This is not a request from the Podman Desktop team, only me ;) . I am having a lot of fun developing [podman-desktop/extension-podman-quadlet](https://github.com/podman-desktop/extension-podman-quadlet) and I would like to be able to do something similar with the pods created from `podman kube play`.
